NKJV.1: Now when they drew near Jerusalem, to Bethphage and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, He sent two of His disciples;
NKJV.2: and He said to them, “Go into the village opposite you; and as soon as you have entered it you will find a colt tied, on which no one has sat. Loose it and bring it.
NKJV.3: And if anyone says to you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ say, ‘The Lord has need of it,’ and immediately he will send it here.”
NKJV.4: So they went their way, and found the colt tied by the door outside on the street, and they loosed it.
NKJV.5: But some of those who stood there said to them, “What are you doing, loosing the colt?”
NKJV.6: And they spoke to them just as Jesus had commanded. So they let them go.
NKJV.7: Then they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes on it, and He sat on it.
NKJV.8: And many spread their clothes on the road, and others cut down leafy branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
NKJV.9: Then those who went before and those who followed cried out, saying: “Hosanna! ‘Blessed is He who comes in the name of the LORD!’
NKJV.10: Blessed is the kingdom of our father David That comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”
NKJV.11: And Jesus went into Jerusalem and into the temple. So when He had looked around at all things, as the hour was already late, He went out to Bethany with the twelve.
NKJV.12: Now the next day, when they had come out from Bethany, He was hungry.
NKJV.13: And seeing from afar a fig tree having leaves, He went to see if perhaps He would find something on it. When He came to it, He found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
NKJV.14: In response Jesus said to it, “Let no one eat fruit from you ever again.” And His disciples heard it.
NKJV.15: So they came to Jerusalem. Then Jesus went into the temple and began to drive out those who bought and sold in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves.
NKJV.16: And He would not allow anyone to carry wares through the temple.
NKJV.17: Then He taught, saying to them, “Is it not written,  ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer for all nations’? But you have made it a  ‘den of thieves.’ ”
NKJV.18: And the scribes and chief priests heard it and sought how they might destroy Him; for they feared Him, because all the people were astonished at His teaching.
NKJV.19: When evening had come, He went out of the city.
NKJV.20: Now in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots.
NKJV.21: And Peter, remembering, said to Him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree which You cursed has withered away.”
NKJV.22: So Jesus answered and said to them, “Have faith in God.
NKJV.23: For  assuredly, I say to you, whoever says to this mountain, ‘Be removed and be cast into the sea,’ and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that those things he says will be done, he will have whatever he says.
NKJV.24: Therefore I say to you, whatever things you ask when you pray, believe that you receive them, and you will have them.
NKJV.25: “And whenever you stand praying,  if you have anything against anyone, forgive him, that your Father in heaven may also forgive you your trespasses.
NKJV.26: But  if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your trespasses.”
NKJV.27: Then they came again to Jerusalem. And as He was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to Him.
NKJV.28: And they said to Him, “By what authority are You doing these things? And who gave You this authority to do these things?”
NKJV.29: But Jesus answered and said to them, “I also will ask you one question; then answer Me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things:
NKJV.30: The  baptism of John—was it from heaven or from men? Answer Me.”
NKJV.31: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ He will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
NKJV.32: But if we say, ‘From men’ ”—they feared the people, for all counted John to have been a prophet indeed.
NKJV.33: So they answered and said to Jesus, “We do not know.” And Jesus answered and said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

ESV.1: Now when they drew near to Jerusalem, to Bethphage and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, Jesus  sent two of his disciples
ESV.2: and said to them, “Go into the village in front of you, and immediately as you enter it you will find a colt tied, on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it.
ESV.3: If anyone says to you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ say, ‘The Lord has need of it and will send it back here immediately.’”
ESV.4: And they went away and found a colt tied at a door outside in the street, and they untied it.
ESV.5: And some of those standing there said to them, “What are you doing, untying the colt?”
ESV.6: And they told them what Jesus had said, and they let them go.
ESV.7: And they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their cloaks on it, and he sat on it.
ESV.8: And many spread their cloaks on the road, and others spread leafy branches that they had cut from the fields.
ESV.9: And those who went before and those who followed were shouting, “Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!
ESV.10: Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David! Hosanna in the highest!”
ESV.11: And he entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. And when he had looked around at everything, as it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.
ESV.12: On the following day, when they came from Bethany, he was hungry.
ESV.13: And seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
ESV.14: And he said to it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again.” And his disciples heard it.
ESV.15: And they came to Jerusalem. And he entered the temple and began to drive out those who sold and those who bought in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money-changers and the seats of those who sold pigeons.
ESV.16: And he would not allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
ESV.17: And he was teaching them and saying to them, “Is it not written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations’? But you have made it a den of robbers.”
ESV.18: And the chief priests and the scribes heard it and were seeking a way to destroy him, for they feared him, because all the crowd was astonished at his teaching.
ESV.19: And when evening came they  went out of the city.
ESV.20: As they passed by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered away to its roots.
ESV.21: And Peter remembered and said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree that you cursed has withered.”
ESV.22: And Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.
ESV.23: Truly, I say to you, whoever says to this mountain, ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea,’ and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says will come to pass, it will be done for him.
ESV.24: Therefore I tell you, whatever you ask in prayer, believe that you have received  it, and it will be yours.
ESV.25: And whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone, so that your Father also who is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.”
ESV.27: And they came again to Jerusalem. And as he was walking in the temple, the chief priests and the scribes and the elders came to him,
ESV.28: and they said to him, “By what authority are you doing these things, or who gave you this authority to do them?”
ESV.29: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question; answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
ESV.30: Was the baptism of John from heaven or from man? Answer me.”
ESV.31: And they discussed it with one another, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
ESV.32: But shall we say, ‘From man’?” — they were afraid of the people, for they all held that John really was a prophet.
ESV.33: So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

NASB.1: As they approached Jerusalem, at Bethphage and Bethany, near the Mount of Olives, He sent two of His disciples,
NASB.2: and said to them, 'Go into the village opposite you, and immediately as you enter it, you will find a colt tied [there], on which no one yet has ever sat; untie it and bring it [here].
NASB.3: 'If anyone says to you, 'Why are you doing this?' you say, 'The Lord has need of it'; and immediately he will send it back here.'
NASB.4: They went away and found a colt tied at the door, outside in the street; and they untied it.
NASB.5: Some of the bystanders were saying to them, 'What are you doing, untying the colt?'
NASB.6: They spoke to them just as Jesus had told [them], and they gave them permission.
NASB.7: They brought the colt to Jesus and put their coats on it; and He sat on it.
NASB.8: And many spread their coats in the road, and others [spread] leafy branches which they had cut from the fields.
NASB.9: Those who went in front and those who followed were shouting: 'Hosanna! BLESSED IS HE WHO COMES IN THE NAME OF THE LORD;
NASB.10: Blessed [is] the coming kingdom of our father David; Hosanna in the highest!'
NASB.11: Jesus entered Jerusalem [and came] into the temple; and after looking around at everything, He left for Bethany with the twelve, since it was already late.
NASB.12: On the next day, when they had left Bethany, He became hungry.
NASB.13: Seeing at a distance a fig tree in leaf, He went [to see] if perhaps He would find anything on it; and when He came to it, He found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
NASB.14: He said to it, 'May no one ever eat fruit from you again!' And His disciples were listening.
NASB.15: Then they came to Jerusalem. And He entered the temple and began to drive out those who were buying and selling in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who were selling doves;
NASB.16: and He would not permit anyone to carry merchandise through the temple.
NASB.17: And He [began] to teach and say to them, 'Is it not written, 'MY HOUSE SHALL BE CALLED A HOUSE OF PRAYER FOR ALL THE NATIONS '? But you have made it a ROBBERS' DEN.'
NASB.18: The chief priests and the scribes heard [this], and [began] seeking how to destroy Him; for they were afraid of Him, for the whole crowd was astonished at His teaching.
NASB.19: When evening came, they would go out of the city.
NASB.20: As they were passing by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered from the roots [up].
NASB.21: Being reminded, Peter said to Him, 'Rabbi, look, the fig tree which You cursed has withered.'
NASB.22: And Jesus answered saying to them, 'Have faith in God.
NASB.23: 'Truly I say to you, whoever says to this mountain, 'Be taken up and cast into the sea,' and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says is going to happen, it will be [granted] him.
NASB.24: 'Therefore I say to you, all things for which you pray and ask, believe that you have received them, and they will be [granted] you.
NASB.25: 'Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone, so that your Father who is in heaven will also forgive you your transgressions.
NASB.26: ['But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father who is in heaven forgive your transgressions.]'
NASB.27: They came again to Jerusalem. And as He was walking in the temple, the chief priests and the scribes and the elders came to Him,
NASB.28: and [began] saying to Him, 'By what authority are You doing these things, or who gave You this authority to do these things?'
NASB.29: And Jesus said to them, 'I will ask you one question, and you answer Me, and [then] I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
NASB.30: 'Was the baptism of John from heaven, or from men? Answer Me.'
NASB.31: They [began] reasoning among themselves, saying, 'If we say, 'From heaven,' He will say, 'Then why did you not believe him?'
NASB.32: 'But shall we say, 'From men '?'-- they were afraid of the people, for everyone considered John to have been a real prophet.
NASB.33: Answering Jesus, they said, 'We do not know.' And Jesus said to them, 'Nor will I tell you by what authority I do these things.'

NLT.1: As Jesus and his disciples approached Jerusalem, they came to the towns of Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives. Jesus sent two of them on ahead.
NLT.2: “Go into that village over there,” he told them. “As soon as you enter it, you will see a young donkey tied there that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
NLT.3: If anyone asks, ‘What are you doing?’ just say, ‘The Lord needs it and will return it soon.’”
NLT.4: The two disciples left and found the colt standing in the street, tied outside the front door.
NLT.5: As they were untying it, some bystanders demanded, “What are you doing, untying that colt?”
NLT.6: They said what Jesus had told them to say, and they were permitted to take it.
NLT.7: Then they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their garments over it, and he sat on it.
NLT.8: Many in the crowd spread their garments on the road ahead of him, and others spread leafy branches they had cut in the fields.
NLT.9:
Jesus was in the center of the procession, and the people all around him were shouting, “Praise God!
Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the LORD!
NLT.10:
Blessings on the coming Kingdom of our ancestor David!
Praise God in highest heaven!”
NLT.11: So Jesus came to Jerusalem and went into the Temple. After looking around carefully at everything, he left because it was late in the afternoon. Then he returned to Bethany with the twelve disciples.
NLT.12: The next morning as they were leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
NLT.13: He noticed a fig tree in full leaf a little way off, so he went over to see if he could find any figs. But there were only leaves because it was too early in the season for fruit.
NLT.14: Then Jesus said to the tree, “May no one ever eat your fruit again!” And the disciples heard him say it.
NLT.15: When they arrived back in Jerusalem, Jesus entered the Temple and began to drive out the people buying and selling animals for sacrifices. He knocked over the tables of the money changers and the chairs of those selling doves,
NLT.16: and he stopped everyone from using the Temple as a marketplace.
NLT.17: He said to them, “The Scriptures declare, ‘My Temple will be called a house of prayer for all nations,’ but you have turned it into a den of thieves.”
NLT.18: When the leading priests and teachers of religious law heard what Jesus had done, they began planning how to kill him. But they were afraid of him because the people were so amazed at his teaching.
NLT.19: That evening Jesus and the disciples left the city.
NLT.20: The next morning as they passed by the fig tree he had cursed, the disciples noticed it had withered from the roots up.
NLT.21: Peter remembered what Jesus had said to the tree on the previous day and exclaimed, “Look, Rabbi! The fig tree you cursed has withered and died!”
NLT.22: Then Jesus said to the disciples, “Have faith in God.
NLT.23: I tell you the truth, you can say to this mountain, ‘May you be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ and it will happen. But you must really believe it will happen and have no doubt in your heart.
NLT.24: I tell you, you can pray for anything, and if you believe that you’ve received it, it will be yours.
NLT.25: But when you are praying, first forgive anyone you are holding a grudge against, so that your Father in heaven will forgive your sins, too. ”
NLT.27: Again they entered Jerusalem. As Jesus was walking through the Temple area, the leading priests, the teachers of religious law, and the elders came up to him.
NLT.28: They demanded, “By what authority are you doing all these things? Who gave you the right to do them?”
NLT.29: “I’ll tell you by what authority I do these things if you answer one question,” Jesus replied.
NLT.30: “Did John’s authority to baptize come from heaven, or was it merely human? Answer me!”
NLT.31: They talked it over among themselves. “If we say it was from heaven, he will ask why we didn’t believe John.
NLT.32: But do we dare say it was merely human?” For they were afraid of what the people would do, because everyone believed that John was a prophet.
NLT.33: So they finally replied, “We don’t know.” And Jesus responded, “Then I won’t tell you by what authority I do these things.”

WEB.1: When they came near to Jerusalem, to Bethsphage  and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,
WEB.2: and said to them, “Go your way into the village that is opposite you. Immediately as you enter into it, you will find a young donkey tied, on which no one has sat. Untie him, and bring him.
WEB.3: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ say, ‘The Lord needs him;’ and immediately he will send him back here.”
WEB.4: They went away, and found a young donkey tied at the door outside in the open street, and they untied him.
WEB.5: Some of those who stood there asked them, “What are you doing, untying the young donkey?”
WEB.6: They said to them just as Jesus had said, and they let them go.
WEB.7: They brought the young donkey to Jesus, and threw their garments on it, and Jesus sat on it.
WEB.8: Many spread their garments on the way, and others were cutting down branches from the trees, and spreading them on the road.
WEB.9: Those who went in front, and those who followed, cried out, “Hosanna!  Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!
WEB.10: Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is coming in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”
WEB.11: Jesus entered into the temple in Jerusalem. When he had looked around at everything, it being now evening, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.
WEB.12: The next day, when they had come out from Bethany, he was hungry.
WEB.13: Seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came to see if perhaps he might find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
WEB.14: Jesus told it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again!”  and his disciples heard it.
WEB.15: They came to Jerusalem, and Jesus entered into the temple, and began to throw out those who sold and those who bought in the temple, and overthrew the money changers’ tables, and the seats of those who sold the doves.
WEB.16: He would not allow anyone to carry a container through the temple.
WEB.17: He taught, saying to them, “Isn’t it written, ‘My house will be called a house of prayer for all the nations?’  But you have made it a den of robbers!”
WEB.18: The chief priests and the scribes heard it, and sought how they might destroy him. For they feared him, because all the multitude was astonished at his teaching.
WEB.19: When evening came, he went out of the city.
WEB.20: As they passed by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered away from the roots.
WEB.21: Peter, remembering, said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree which you cursed has withered away.”
WEB.22: Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.
WEB.23: For most certainly I tell you, whoever may tell this mountain, ‘Be taken up and cast into the sea,’ and doesn’t doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says is happening; he shall have whatever he says.
WEB.24: Therefore I tell you, all things whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you have received them, and you shall have them.
WEB.25: Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father, who is in heaven, may also forgive you your transgressions.
WEB.26: But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your transgressions.”
WEB.27: They came again to Jerusalem, and as he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders came to him,
WEB.28: and they began saying to him, “By what authority do you do these things? Or who gave you this authority to do these things?”
WEB.29: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question. Answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
WEB.30: The baptism of John — was it from heaven, or from men? Answer me.”
WEB.31: They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we should say, ‘From heaven;’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
WEB.32: If we should say, ‘From men’” — they feared the people, for all held John to really be a prophet.
WEB.33: They answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” Jesus said to them, “Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

CSB17.1: When <sup>ⓗ</sup> they approached Jerusalem, <sup>ⓘ</sup> at Bethphage and Bethany <sup>ⓙ</sup> near the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples
CSB17.2: and told them, “Go into the village ahead of you. As soon as you enter it, you will find a colt tied there, on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it.
CSB17.3: If anyone says to you, ‘Why are you doing this? ’ say, ‘The Lord needs it and will send it back here right away.’”
CSB17.4: So they went and found a colt outside in the street, tied by a door. They untied it,
CSB17.5: and some of those standing there said to them, “What are you doing, untying the colt?”
CSB17.6: They answered them just as Jesus had said; so they let them go.
CSB17.7: They brought the donkey to Jesus and threw their clothes on it, and he sat on it.
CSB17.8: Many people spread their clothes on the road, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and others spread leafy branches cut from the fields. <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.9:
Those who went ahead and those who followed shouted: Hosanna!
Blessed is he who comes
in the name ⓛ ⓜ of the Lord! [2] ⓝ
CSB17.10:
Blessed is the coming kingdom ⓐ
of our father David! ⓑ
Hosanna ⓒ in the highest heaven!
CSB17.11: He went into Jerusalem <sup>ⓓ</sup> and into the temple. <sup>ⓔ</sup> After looking around at everything, since it was already late, he went out to Bethany <sup>ⓕ</sup> with the Twelve.
CSB17.12: The <sup>ⓖ</sup> next day when they went out from Bethany, he was hungry.
CSB17.13: Seeing in the distance a fig <sup>ⓗ</sup> tree with leaves, he went to find out if there was anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves; for it was not the season for figs.
CSB17.14: He said to it, “May no one ever eat fruit <sup>ⓘ</sup> from you again!” <sup>ⓙ</sup> And his disciples <sup>ⓚ</sup> heard it.
CSB17.15: They came to Jerusalem, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and he went into the temple <sup>ⓜ</sup> and began to throw out those buying and selling. He overturned the tables of the money changers <sup>ⓝ</sup> and the chairs of those selling doves,
CSB17.16: and would not permit anyone to carry goods through the temple.
CSB17.17: He was teaching them: “Is it not written, ⓐ My house ⓑ will be called a house of prayer ⓒ for all nations? ⓓ But you have made it a den of thieves!” [3] ⓔ
CSB17.18: The chief priests and the scribes <sup>ⓕ</sup> heard it and started looking for a way to kill him. For they were afraid <sup>ⓖ</sup> of him, because the whole crowd was astonished by his teaching.
CSB17.19: Whenever evening came, they would go out of the city.
CSB17.20: Early <sup>ⓗ</sup> in the morning, as they were passing by, they saw the fig tree withered from the roots up.
CSB17.21: Then Peter <sup>ⓘ</sup> remembered and said to him, “Rabbi, <sup>ⓙ</sup> look! The fig tree that you cursed <sup>ⓚ</sup> has withered.”
CSB17.22: Jesus replied to them, “Have faith in God. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.23: Truly I tell you, <sup>ⓜ</sup> if anyone says to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ and does not doubt <sup>ⓝ</sup> in his heart, but believes <sup>ⓐ</sup> that what he says will happen, it will be done for him. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.24: Therefore I tell you, everything you pray <sup>ⓒ</sup>and ask for <sup>ⓓ</sup>  —  believe that you have received <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup>it and it will be yours.
CSB17.25: And whenever you stand <sup>ⓕ</sup> praying, if you have anything against anyone, forgive <sup>ⓖ</sup> him, so that your Father <sup>ⓗ</sup> in heaven <sup>ⓘ</sup> will also forgive <sup>ⓙ</sup> you your wrongdoing.” <sup>[5]</sup>
CSB17.27: They <sup>ⓚ</sup> came again to Jerusalem. <sup>ⓛ</sup> As he was walking in the temple, <sup>ⓜ</sup> the chief priests, the scribes, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and the elders <sup>ⓐ</sup> came
CSB17.28: and asked him, “By what authority <sup>ⓑ</sup> are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do these things?”
CSB17.29: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question; <sup>ⓒ</sup> then answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
CSB17.30: Was John’s baptism <sup>ⓓ</sup> from heaven or of human origin? Answer me.”
CSB17.31: They discussed it among themselves: “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Then why didn’t you believe <sup>ⓔ</sup> him?’
CSB17.32: But if we say, ‘Of human origin’” — they were afraid of the crowd, because everyone thought that John was truly a prophet. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.33: So they answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

AMP.1: When they were nearing Jerusalem, at Bethphage and Bethany near the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two of His disciples,
AMP.2: saying to them, "Go into the village in front of you, and immediately as you enter it, you will find a [donkey's] colt tied, which has never been ridden by anyone; untie it and bring it here.
AMP.3: If anyone asks you, 'Why are you doing this?' say, 'The Lord needs it'; and immediately he will send it here."
AMP.4: So they went away [to the village] and found a colt tied outside at a gate in the street, and they untied it.
AMP.5: Some of the people who were standing there said to them, "What are you doing, untying the colt?"
AMP.6: They replied to them just as Jesus had directed, and they allowed them to go.
AMP.7: They brought the colt to Jesus and put their coats on it, and He sat on it.
AMP.8: And many [of the people] spread their coats on the road [as an act of tribute and homage before a new king], and others [scattered a layer of] leafy branches which they had cut from the fields [honoring Him as Messiah].
AMP.9:
Those who went in front and those who were following [Him] were shouting [in joy and praise],
"Hosanna ( Save, I pray)!
Blessed (praised, glorified) is He who comes in the name of the Lord!
AMP.10:
"Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David!
Hosanna in the highest [heaven]!"
AMP.11: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went to the temple [enclosure]; and after looking around at everything, He left for Bethany with the twelve [disciples], because it was already late [in the day].
AMP.12: On the next day, when they had left Bethany, He was hungry.
AMP.13: Seeing at a distance a fig tree in leaf, He went to see if He would find anything on it. But He found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
AMP.14: He said to it, "No one will ever eat fruit from you again!" And His disciples were listening [to what He said].
AMP.15: Then they came to Jerusalem. And He entered the temple [grounds] and began driving out [with force] the people who were selling and buying [animals for sacrifice] in the temple area, and overturned the tables of the moneychangers [who made a profit exchanging foreign money for temple coinage] and the seats of those who were selling doves;
AMP.16: and He would not permit anyone to carry merchandise or household wares through the temple [grounds, using the temple area irreverently as a shortcut].
AMP.17: He began to teach and say to them, "Is it not written, 'My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations'? But you have made it a robbers' den."
AMP.18: The chief priests and the scribes heard this and began searching for a way to destroy Him; for they were afraid of Him, since the entire crowd was struck with astonishment at His teaching.
AMP.19: When evening came, Jesus and His disciples would leave the city.
AMP.20: In the morning, as they were passing by, the disciples saw that the fig tree had withered away from the roots up.
AMP.21: And remembering, Peter said to Him, "Rabbi (Master), look! The fig tree which You cursed has withered!"
AMP.23: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, whoever says to this mountain, 'Be lifted up and thrown into the sea!' and does not doubt in his heart [in God's unlimited power], but believes that what he says is going to take place, it will be done for him [in accordance with God's will].
AMP.24: For this reason I am telling you, whatever things you ask for in prayer [in accordance with God's will], believe [with confident trust] that you have received them, and they will be given to you.
AMP.25: Whenever you stand praying, if you have anything against anyone, forgive him [drop the issue, let it go], so that your Father who is in heaven will also forgive you your transgressions and wrongdoings [against Him and others].
AMP.26: ["But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your transgressions."]
AMP.27: They came again to Jerusalem. And as Jesus was walking in the [courts and porches of the] temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to Him,
AMP.28: and began saying to Him, "By what authority are You doing these things, or who gave You this authority to do these things?"
AMP.29: Jesus said to them, "I will ask you one question, and you answer Me, and then I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
AMP.30: Was the baptism of John [the Baptist] from heaven [that is, ordained by God] or from men? Answer Me."
AMP.31: They began discussing it with each other, saying, "If we say, 'From heaven,' He will say, 'Then why did you not believe him?'
AMP.32: But shall we say, 'From men?'" —they were afraid [to answer because] of the crowd, for everyone considered John to have been a real prophet.
AMP.33: So they replied to Jesus, "We do not know." And Jesus said to them, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things."

MSG.1: When they were nearing Jerusalem, at Bethphage and Bethany on Mount Olives, he sent off two of the disciples with instructions:
MSG.2: "Go to the village across from you. As soon as you enter, you'll find a colt tethered, one that has never yet been ridden. Untie it and bring it.
MSG.3: If anyone asks, 'What are you doing?' say, 'The Master needs him, and will return him right away.'"
MSG.4: They went and found a colt tied to a door at the street corner and untied it.
MSG.5: Some of those standing there said, "What are you doing untying that colt?"
MSG.6: The disciples replied exactly as Jesus had instructed them, and the people let them alone.
MSG.7: They brought the colt to Jesus, spread their coats on it, and he mounted.
MSG.8: The people gave him a wonderful welcome, some throwing their coats on the street, others spreading out rushes they had cut in the fields.
MSG.9: Running ahead and following after, they were calling out, Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in God's name!
MSG.10: Blessed the coming kingdom of our father David! Hosanna in highest heaven!
MSG.11: He entered Jerusalem, then entered the Temple. He looked around, taking it all in. But by now it was late, so he went back to Bethany with the Twelve.
MSG.12: As they left Bethany the next day, he was hungry.
MSG.13: Off in the distance he saw a fig tree in full leaf. He came up to it expecting to find something for breakfast, but found nothing but fig leaves. (It wasn't yet the season for figs.)
MSG.14: He addressed the tree: "No one is going to eat fruit from you again—ever!" And his disciples overheard him.
MSG.15: They arrived at Jerusalem. Immediately on entering the Temple Jesus started throwing out everyone who had set up shop there, buying and selling. He kicked over the tables of the bankers and the stalls of the pigeon merchants.
MSG.16: He didn't let anyone even carry a basket through the Temple.
MSG.17: And then he taught them, quoting this text: My house was designated a house of prayer for the nations; You've turned it into a hangout for thieves.
MSG.18: The high priests and religion scholars heard what was going on and plotted how they might get rid of him. They panicked, for the entire crowd was carried away by his teaching.
MSG.19: At evening, Jesus and his disciples left the city.
MSG.20: In the morning, walking along the road, they saw the fig tree, shriveled to a dry stick.
MSG.21: Peter, remembering what had happened the previous day, said to him, "Rabbi, look—the fig tree you cursed is shriveled up!"
MSG.22: Jesus was matter-of-fact: "Embrace this God-life. Really embrace it,
MSG.23: and nothing will be too much for you. This mountain, for instance: Just say, 'Go jump in the lake'—no shuffling or shilly-shallying—and it's as good as done.
MSG.24: That's why I urge you to pray for absolutely everything, ranging from small to large. Include everything as you embrace this God-life, and you'll get God's everything.
MSG.25: And when you assume the posture of prayer, remember that it's not all asking. If you have anything against someone, forgive—only then will your heavenly Father be inclined to also wipe your slate clean of sins."
MSG.27: Then when they were back in Jerusalem once again, as they were walking through the Temple, the high priests, religion scholars, and leaders came up
MSG.28: and demanded, "Show us your credentials. Who authorized you to speak and act like this?"
MSG.29: Jesus responded, "First let me ask you a question. Answer my question and then I'll present my credentials.
MSG.30: About the baptism of John—who authorized it: heaven or humans? Tell me."
MSG.31: They were on the spot, and knew it. They pulled back into a huddle and whispered, "If we say 'heaven,' he'll ask us why we didn't believe John;
MSG.32: if we say 'humans,' we'll be up against it with the people because they all hold John up as a prophet."
MSG.33: They decided to concede that round to Jesus. "We don't know," they said. Jesus replied, "Then I won't answer your question either."

NIV.1:
The Triumphal Entry
As they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two of his disciples,
NIV.2: saying to them, “Go to the village ahead of you, and just as you enter it, you will find a colt tied there, which no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
NIV.3: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ tell him, ‘The Lord needs it and will send it back here shortly.’”
NIV.4: They went and found a colt outside in the street, tied at a doorway. As they untied it,
NIV.5: some people standing there asked, “What are you doing, untying that colt?”
NIV.6: They answered as Jesus had told them to, and the people let them go.
NIV.7: When they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their cloaks over it, he sat on it.
NIV.8: Many people spread their cloaks on the road, while others spread branches they had cut in the fields.
NIV.9:
Those who went ahead and those who followed shouted,
“Hosanna!”
“Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!”
NIV.10:
“Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David!”
“Hosanna in the highest!”
NIV.11: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went to the temple. He looked around at everything, but since it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the Twelve.
NIV.12:
Jesus Clears the Temple
The next day as they were leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
NIV.13: Seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, he went to find out if it had any fruit. When he reached it, he found nothing but leaves, because it was not the season for figs.
NIV.14: Then he said to the tree, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again.” And his disciples heard him say it.
NIV.15: On reaching Jerusalem, Jesus entered the temple area and began driving out those who were buying and selling there. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the benches of those selling doves,
NIV.16: and would not allow anyone to carry merchandise through the temple courts.
NIV.17:
And as he taught them, he said, “Is it not written:
“‘My house will be called
a house of prayer for all nations’?
But you have made it ‘a den of robbers.’”
NIV.18: The chief priests and the teachers of the law heard this and began looking for a way to kill him, for they feared him, because the whole crowd was amazed at his teaching.
NIV.19: When evening came, they went out of the city.
NIV.20:
The Withered Fig Tree
In the morning, as they went along, they saw the fig tree withered from the roots.
NIV.21: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree you cursed has withered!”
NIV.23: “I tell you the truth, if anyone says to this mountain, ‘Go, throw yourself into the sea,’ and does not doubt in his heart but believes that what he says will happen, it will be done for him.
NIV.24: Therefore I tell you, whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
NIV.25: And when you stand praying, if you hold anything against anyone, forgive him, so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your sins.”
NIV.27:
The Authority of Jesus Questioned
They arrived again in Jerusalem, and while Jesus was walking in the temple courts, the chief priests, the teachers of the law and the elders came to him.
NIV.28: “By what authority are you doing these things?” they asked. “And who gave you authority to do this?”
NIV.29: Jesus replied, “I will ask you one question. Answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
NIV.30: John’s baptism—was it from heaven, or from men? Tell me!”
NIV.31: They discussed it among themselves and said, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will ask, ‘Then why didn’t you believe him?’
NIV.32: But if we say, ‘From men’…” (They feared the people, for everyone held that John really was a prophet.)
NIV.33:
So they answered Jesus, “We don’t know.”
Jesus said, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”

NRSVCE.1: When they were approaching Jerusalem, at Bethphage and Bethany, near the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples
NRSVCE.2: and said to them, “Go into the village ahead of you, and immediately as you enter it, you will find tied there a colt that has never been ridden; untie it and bring it.
NRSVCE.3: If anyone says to you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ just say this, ‘The Lord needs it and will send it back here immediately.’”
NRSVCE.4: They went away and found a colt tied near a door, outside in the street. As they were untying it,
NRSVCE.5: some of the bystanders said to them, “What are you doing, untying the colt?”
NRSVCE.6: They told them what Jesus had said; and they allowed them to take it.
NRSVCE.7: Then they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their cloaks on it; and he sat on it.
NRSVCE.8: Many people spread their cloaks on the road, and others spread leafy branches that they had cut in the fields.
NRSVCE.9:
Then those who went ahead and those who followed were shouting, “Hosanna!
Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
NRSVCE.10:
Blessed is the coming kingdom of our ancestor David!
Hosanna in the highest heaven!”
NRSVCE.11: Then he entered Jerusalem and went into the temple; and when he had looked around at everything, as it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.
NRSVCE.12: On the following day, when they came from Bethany, he was hungry.
NRSVCE.13: Seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, he went to see whether perhaps he would find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
NRSVCE.14: He said to it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again.” And his disciples heard it.
NRSVCE.15: Then they came to Jerusalem. And he entered the temple and began to drive out those who were selling and those who were buying in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves;
NRSVCE.16: and he would not allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
NRSVCE.17:
He was teaching and saying, “Is it not written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations’?
But you have made it a den of robbers.”
NRSVCE.18: And when the chief priests and the scribes heard it, they kept looking for a way to kill him; for they were afraid of him, because the whole crowd was spellbound by his teaching.
NRSVCE.19: And when evening came, Jesus and his disciples went out of the city.
NRSVCE.20: In the morning as they passed by, they saw the fig tree withered away to its roots.
NRSVCE.21: Then Peter remembered and said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree that you cursed has withered.”
NRSVCE.22: Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.
NRSVCE.23: Truly I tell you, if you say to this mountain, ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea,’ and if you do not doubt in your heart, but believe that what you say will come to pass, it will be done for you.
NRSVCE.24: So I tell you, whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
NRSVCE.25: “Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father in heaven may also forgive you your trespasses.”
NRSVCE.27: Again they came to Jerusalem. As he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to him
NRSVCE.28: and said, “By what authority are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them?”
NRSVCE.29: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question; answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
NRSVCE.30: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin? Answer me.”
NRSVCE.31: They argued with one another, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
NRSVCE.32: But shall we say, ‘Of human origin’?” — they were afraid of the crowd, for all regarded John as truly a prophet.
NRSVCE.33: So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”

NET.1: Now as they approached Jerusalem, near Bethphage and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two of his disciples
NET.2: and said to them, “Go to the village ahead of you. As soon as you enter it, you will find a colt tied there that has never been ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
NET.3: If anyone says to you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ say, ‘The Lord needs it and will send it back here soon.’”
NET.4: So they went and found a colt tied at a door, outside in the street, and untied it.
NET.5: Some people standing there said to them, “What are you doing, untying that colt?”
NET.6: They replied as Jesus had told them, and the bystanders let them go.
NET.7: Then they brought the colt to Jesus, threw their cloaks on it, and he sat on it.
NET.8: Many spread their cloaks on the road and others spread branches they had cut in the fields.
NET.9: Both those who went ahead and those who followed kept shouting, “Hosanna! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
NET.10: Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David! Hosanna in the highest!”
NET.11: Then Jesus entered Jerusalem and went to the temple. And after looking around at everything, he went out to Bethany with the twelve since it was already late.
NET.12: Now the next day, as they went out from Bethany, he was hungry.
NET.13: After noticing in the distance a fig tree with leaves, he went to see if he could find any fruit on it. When he came to it he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
NET.14: He said to it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again.” And his disciples heard it.
NET.15: Then they came to Jerusalem. Jesus entered the temple area and began to drive out those who were selling and buying in the temple courts. He turned over the tables of the money changers and the chairs of those selling doves,
NET.16: and he would not permit anyone to carry merchandise through the temple courts.
NET.17: Then he began to teach them and said, “Is it not written: ‘My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations’? But you have turned it into a den of robbers!”
NET.18: The chief priests and the experts in the law heard it and they considered how they could assassinate him, for they feared him, because the whole crowd was amazed by his teaching.
NET.19: When evening came, Jesus and his disciples went out of the city.
NET.20: In the morning as they passed by, they saw the fig tree withered from the roots.
NET.21: Peter remembered and said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree you cursed has withered.”
NET.22: Jesus said to them, “Have faith in God.
NET.23: I tell you the truth, if someone says to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ and does not doubt in his heart but believes that what he says will happen, it will be done for him.
NET.24: For this reason I tell you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
NET.25: Whenever you stand praying, if you have anything against anyone, forgive him, so that your Father in heaven will also forgive you your sins.”
NET.27: They came again to Jerusalem. While Jesus was walking in the temple courts, the chief priests, the experts in the law, and the elders came up to him
NET.28: and said, “By what authority are you doing these things? Or who gave you this authority to do these things?”
NET.29: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question. Answer me and I will tell you by what authority I do these things:
NET.30: John’s baptism — was it from heaven or from people? Answer me.”
NET.31: They discussed with one another, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Then why did you not believe him?’
NET.32: But if we say, ‘From people — ’” (they feared the crowd, for they all considered John to be truly a prophet).
NET.33: So they answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” Then Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”
